personality: 3 Editorial structure with 6 Glass / Luxe surfaces
personality_one_liner: A cinematic, deeply personal documentary journey blending NatGeo-style breathtaking photography, elegant typography, and profound storytelling.

spacing.rule: Editorial breathing room. Avoid dense clusters; give photos and text ample margins. Keep safe area clearances.

do's: Use heavy glass with a dark translucent base for sticky headers over scrolling photos.
Use blur on the bottom tab bar to let maps and gallery items peek through.
Use subtle glass cards layered over large cinematic background imagery.
dont's: Don't stack glass on top of glass.
Don't use glass without a strong gradient scrim guaranteeing text readability.

screens.name: Home
screens.purpose: Cinematic landing telling the overarching story of a 40-year dream spanning 20,000 km.

states.loading: Centered minimalist spinner in gold on a deep navy background.
states.error: Elegant error message stating 'The journey is momentarily paused. Please reconnect.' with a 'Retry' button.

screens.layout: Full-bleed hero image of a cinematic train window with a top-to-bottom dark gradient scrim. The display title 'Alberto 100 Carris' in large serif overlay. Below the fold, story blocks featuring 'The History' and 'Purpose' utilizing alternating text and edge-to-edge photography. Bottom floating CTA button: 'Começar a viagem' (Start Journey).

screens.name: Map (Mapa)
screens.purpose: Interactive geographical exploration of the 16 connected countries.
screens.layout: Dark-themed interactive map with a glowing gold route line connecting countries. Tapping a country pulls up a bottom sheet (glass header) showcasing country-specific curiosities, a timeline date, and horizontal scrollable photos.

screens.name: Diary (Diário)
screens.purpose: Chronological travel journal documenting daily experiences.

states.empty: Illustration of a vintage travel diary waiting to be written.

screens.layout: Editorial list view. Each entry card acts like a magazine snippet: a large hero photo, a date/location subheader, a short snippet of text in DM Sans, and weather/distance metadata styled elegantly.

screens.name: More (Mais)
screens.purpose: Hub for videos, gallery, live stats, equipment, and settings (language toggle).
screens.layout: Grid or list of premium glass-effect tiles over a subtle abstract background. Live statistics dashboard prominent at the top with large gold numbers (Cormorant Garamond) over navy cards.

hero_train_window.alt: View from a train window at sunset

hero_mountain_river.alt: Peaceful mountain and river scene viewed through a train window

country_russia_transsiberian.alt: Approaching red train across mountains covered in snow

country_singapore_skyline.alt: Singapore night skyline and Marina Bay Sands

empty_diary.alt: Rustic leather journal on a desk

instructions_to_main_agent: Strictly adhere to the dark 'cinematic' color palette using Deep Navy (#050B14) as the primary base and Champagne Gold (#E6C365) as the primary accent.
Typography MUST use Cormorant Garamond for all headings, display titles, and large numbers, paired with DM Sans for body copy and metadata. Keep font weights at 400 or 500 max.
Implement a persistent PT/EN language toggle pill (glass effect or subtle surfaceTertiary background).
All full-bleed hero images and background images MUST include a vertical linear gradient scrim (transparent to #050B14) to guarantee text readability.
For the Map screen, use an interactive map library styled darkly, with a gold glowing line indicating the train route and tappable markers for the 16 countries.
The Diary screen should act like an editorial layout: generous padding, edge-to-edge images in cards, beautiful serif dates, and a clear visual hierarchy.
Include subtle motion: parallax scrolling on the hero image, fade-in for diary list items, and smooth bottom-sheet transitions.
Never use generic blue/purple UI aesthetics; lean into the custom Deep Navy and Gold.
Ensure touch targets are a minimum of 44x44pt.
